interactions.summary: Jiang reframes Trump as a mafia boss whose consistency lies in personal advantage and television appearance rather than national interest.
The host asks for final thoughts on 2026.
Jiang closes by forecasting more pressure on China across South America, Africa, and Japan before an April arrangement that clears the way toward the Iran endgame.
The host closes on the image that whether elephants fight or mate, the grass still gets flattened.

claims.claim: Jiang says Trump is best understood as a mafia boss who acts for personal benefit and image rather than the national interest of the United States.
Jiang predicts more US pressure across South America, Africa, and the China-Japan theater before the April China meeting.
Jiang says Trump needs to settle the China issue before a full-scale invasion of Iran because Russia would defend Iran and China remains the wild card.
Jiang predicts that despite surprise, China and the United States will come to an arrangement before the larger Iran endgame unfolds.

signature_moments.moment: Consistency is relocated from statecraft to organized-crime logic.
signature_moments.source_phrase: He’s not the president of the United States. He’s a mafia boss
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the interview’s bluntest closing definition of Trump’s operating model.

signature_moments.moment: Whether the great powers clash or bargain, smaller actors still get crushed.
signature_moments.source_phrase: when the elephants fight ... when the elephants make love, it also gets flattened
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the closing arrangement forecast a tragic rather than conciliatory frame.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang uses this as the key interpretive model for Trump’s consistency, bargaining style, and indifference to national interest.

chronology_notes.note: The closing chronology runs from escalating leverage before April, to a China arrangement, to the later Iran endgame.

uncertainty_notes.note: The forecast that China and the United States will come to an arrangement is Jiang’s stated bet, not a transcript-demonstrated outcome.
